Document Properties

Complete documentation for document properties in Aspose.Cells FOSS for .NET

← Back to Index

Document Properties Overview

Document properties (metadata) provide information about your Excel workbooks, such as title, author, creation date, and custom properties. These properties help organize documents, improve searchability, and provide context about the workbook's purpose and origin.

Property Types:
  • Core Properties: Standard metadata like title, author, subject, keywords, etc.
  • Extended Properties: Application-specific information like application name, version, etc.
  • Custom Properties: User-defined properties for specific needs

Accessing Document Properties

Accessing Properties
var workbook = new Workbook();

// Access document properties
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

// Access through different entry points
var properties2 = workbook.Properties;  // Alternative access
Core vs Extended Properties
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

// Core properties (standard metadata)
var coreProperties = properties.Core;

// Extended properties (application-specific)
var extendedProperties = properties.Extended;

Core Properties

Basic Core Properties
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

// Title
properties.Title = "Annual Financial Report";
string title = properties.Title;

// Author
properties.Author = "John Doe";
string author = properties.Author;

// Subject
properties.Subject = "Financial Summary for FY2026";
string subject = properties.Subject;

// Keywords
properties.Keywords = "financial, report, summary, 2026";
string keywords = properties.Keywords;

// Comments
properties.Comments = "Prepared by the Finance Department. Generated using Aspose.Cells FOSS.";
string comments = properties.Comments;

// Company
properties.Company = "ABC Corporation";
string company = properties.Company;

// Category
properties.Category = "Finance";
string category = properties.Category;
Manager and Status
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

// Manager (document manager)
properties.Manager = "Jane Smith";
string manager = properties.Manager;

// Status (document status)
properties.Status = "Final";
string status = properties.Status;

// Hyperlink base (for relative hyperlinks)
properties.HyperlinkBase = "https://www.example.com/documents/";
string hyperlinkBase = properties.HyperlinkBase;

Extended Properties

Application Information
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;
var extended = properties.Extended;

// Application that created the document
extended.Application = "Aspose.Cells FOSS";
string application = extended.Application;

// Document manager
extended.Manager = "Document Management System";
string manager = extended.Manager;

// Company
extended.Company = "ABC Corporation";
string company = extended.Company;

// Version
extended.Version = "1.0";
string version = extended.Version;
Document Statistics
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;
var extended = properties.Extended;

// Number of pages (calculated)
int pages = extended.Pages;

// Number of words (calculated)
int words = extended.Words;

// Number of characters (calculated)
int characters = extended.Characters;

// Number of paragraphs (calculated)
int paragraphs = extended.Paragraphs;

// Note: These statistics are automatically calculated by Excel
Security Settings
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;
var extended = properties.Extended;

// Security classification
extended.Security = "Internal";
string security = extended.Security;

// Document template
extended.Template = "Standard Financial Report";
string template = extended.Template;

Custom Properties

Adding Custom Properties
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

// Add custom property - String
properties.Add("ProjectCode", "PRJ-2026-001");

// Add custom property - Integer
properties.Add("ReviewCount", 3);

// Add custom property - DateTime
properties.Add("ReviewDate", DateTime.Now);

// Add custom property - Boolean
properties.Add("Approved", true);

// Add custom property - Double
properties.Add("Budget", 50000.00);
Reading Custom Properties
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

// Get property by name
if (properties.Contains("ProjectCode"))
{
    string projectCode = properties["ProjectCode"].Value as string;
    Console.WriteLine($"Project Code: {projectCode}");
}

// Get property value directly
object reviewCount = properties["ReviewCount"].Value;
object reviewDate = properties["ReviewDate"].Value;
object approved = properties["Approved"].Value;
object budget = properties["Budget"].Value;
Updating Custom Properties
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

// Update existing custom property
properties["ReviewCount"].Value = 4;
properties["ReviewDate"].Value = DateTime.Now.AddDays(5);
properties["Approved"].Value = false;

// Or remove and re-add
properties.Remove("ProjectCode");
properties.Add("ProjectCode", "PRJ-2026-002");
Listing Custom Properties
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

// Iterate through all custom properties
foreach (var property in properties)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Name: {property.Name}");
    Console.WriteLine($"Type: {property.Type}");
    Console.WriteLine($"Value: {property.Value}");
    Console.WriteLine();
}

// Get count
int customPropertyCount = properties.Count;
Removing Custom Properties
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

// Remove specific property
properties.Remove("ProjectCode");

// Remove all custom properties
properties.Clear();

Property Types

Available Data Types
// Supported data types for custom properties:
// - String (text)
properties.Add("Department", "Finance");

// - Integer (32-bit)
properties.Add("Version", 1);
properties.Add("Year", 2026);

// - Double (floating point)
properties.Add("Amount", 1234.56);

// - Boolean (true/false)
properties.Add("Archived", true);
properties.Add("Published", false);

// - DateTime (date and time)
properties.Add("CreatedDate", DateTime.Now);
properties.Add("DueDate", new DateTime(2026, 12, 31));
Type Checking
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

// Check property type
var property = properties["ProjectCode"];
PropertyType type = property.Type;

switch (type)
{
    case PropertyType.String:
        Console.WriteLine("String property");
        break;
    case PropertyType.Int:
        Console.WriteLine("Integer property");
        break;
    case PropertyType.Double:
        Console.WriteLine("Double property");
        break;
    case PropertyType.Boolean:
        Console.WriteLine("Boolean property");
        break;
    case PropertyType.DateTime:
        Console.WriteLine("DateTime property");
        break;
}

Best Practices

Property Management
// Always check if property exists before accessing
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

if (properties.Contains("ProjectCode"))
{
    string code = properties["ProjectCode"].Value as string;
    // Use the property
}
else
{
    Console.WriteLine("ProjectCode property not found");
}

// Use descriptive property names
properties.Add("ProjectCode", "PRJ-001");        // Good
properties.Add("ReviewDate", DateTime.Now);      // Good
properties.Add("p1", "value1");                 // Bad - not descriptive

// Use appropriate data types
properties.Add("ReviewCount", 3);               // Good - integer
properties.Add("ReviewCount", "3");              // Avoid - string for number

// Keep property names consistent
properties.Add("ProjectCode", "PRJ-001");       // camelCase
properties.Add("ReviewDate", DateTime.Now);      // camelCase
properties.Add("Approved", true);               // camelCase
Property Validation
// Validate property values before setting
var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

// Validate project code format
string projectCode = "PRJ-2026-001";
if (projectCode.StartsWith("PRJ-"))
{
    properties.Add("ProjectCode", projectCode);
}
else
{
    Console.WriteLine("Invalid project code format");
}

// Validate date range
DateTime dueDate = DateTime.Now.AddMonths(6);
if (dueDate > DateTime.Now)
{
    properties.Add("DueDate", dueDate);
}
else
{
    Console.WriteLine("Due date must be in the future");
}

Complete Example

Document Metadata Setup
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var workbook = new Workbook();
        var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

        // Set core properties
        properties.Title = "Annual Financial Report 2026";
        properties.Author = "John Doe";
        properties.Subject = "Comprehensive financial analysis for fiscal year 2026";
        properties.Keywords = "finance, report, annual, 2026, revenue, expenses, profit";
        properties.Comments = "Prepared by Finance Department. Version 1.0. Final approved version.";
        properties.Company = "ABC Corporation";
        properties.Category = "Financial Reports";
        properties.Manager = "Jane Smith";
        properties.Status = "Final";
        properties.HyperlinkBase = "https://www.abccorp.com/reports/2026/";

        // Set extended properties
        var extended = properties.Extended;
        extended.Application = "Aspose.Cells FOSS";
        extended.Manager = "Document Management System";
        extended.Company = "ABC Corporation";
        extended.Version = "1.0";
        extended.Template = "Standard Financial Report Template v2.0";
        extended.Security = "Internal Use Only";

        // Add custom properties
        properties.Add("ProjectCode", "FIN-2026-001");
        properties.Add("ReportType", "Annual");
        properties.Add("FiscalYear", 2026);
        properties.Add("Currency", "USD");
        properties.Add("PreparedDate", DateTime.Now);
        properties.Add("ReviewDate", DateTime.Now.AddDays(-5));
        properties.Add("ApprovedDate", DateTime.Now);
        properties.Add("ApprovedBy", "Jane Smith");
        properties.Add("ApproverTitle", "CFO");
        properties.Add("ReviewCycle", 3);
        properties.Add("DataSources", 5);
        properties.Add("Confidential", true);
        properties.Add("PublicRelease", false);
        properties.Add("BudgetAllocation", 150000.00);
        properties.Add("ActualSpending", 145500.00);
        properties.Add("VariancePercentage", 3.0);

        // Display all properties
        Console.WriteLine("=== Document Properties ===");
        Console.WriteLine($"Title: {properties.Title}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Author: {properties.Author}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Subject: {properties.Subject}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Company: {properties.Company}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Status: {properties.Status}");
        Console.WriteLine();

        Console.WriteLine("=== Custom Properties ===");
        foreach (var property in properties)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"{property.Name}: {property.Value} ({property.Type})");
        }

        // Save workbook
        workbook.Save("financial-report-with-metadata.xlsx");
        Console.WriteLine();
        Console.WriteLine("Workbook saved with complete metadata!");
    }
}
Reading Document Properties
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var workbook = new Workbook("financial-report-with-metadata.xlsx");
        var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

        // Read core properties
        Console.WriteLine("=== Core Properties ===");
        Console.WriteLine($"Title: {properties.Title}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Author: {properties.Author}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Subject: {properties.Subject}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Keywords: {properties.Keywords}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Comments: {properties.Comments}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Company: {properties.Company}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Category: {properties.Category}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Manager: {properties.Manager}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Status: {properties.Status}");
        Console.WriteLine();

        // Read extended properties
        Console.WriteLine("=== Extended Properties ===");
        var extended = properties.Extended;
        Console.WriteLine($"Application: {extended.Application}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Version: {extended.Version}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Template: {extended.Template}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Security: {extended.Security}");
        Console.WriteLine();

        // Read custom properties
        Console.WriteLine("=== Custom Properties ===");
        if (properties.Count > 0)
        {
            foreach (var property in properties)
            {
                string valueDisplay;
                switch (property.Type)
                {
                    case PropertyType.DateTime:
                        valueDisplay = ((DateTime)property.Value).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss");
                        break;
                    case PropertyType.Double:
                        valueDisplay = ((double)property.Value).ToString("F2");
                        break;
                    default:
                        valueDisplay = property.Value?.ToString() ?? "null";
                        break;
                }
                Console.WriteLine($"{property.Name}: {valueDisplay}");
            }
        }
        else
        {
            Console.WriteLine("No custom properties found.");
        }

        // Check specific properties
        Console.WriteLine();
        Console.WriteLine("=== Specific Property Checks ===");

        if (properties.Contains("ProjectCode"))
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"Project Code: {properties["ProjectCode"].Value}");
        }

        if (properties.Contains("Approved"))
        {
            bool approved = (bool)properties["Approved"].Value;
            Console.WriteLine($"Approved: {(approved ? "Yes" : "No")}");
        }

        if (properties.Contains("ReviewCycle"))
        {
            int cycles = (int)properties["ReviewCycle"].Value;
            Console.WriteLine($"Review Cycles: {cycles}");
        }

        Console.WriteLine();
        Console.WriteLine("Document properties read successfully!");
    }
}
Property Search and Filtering
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;
using System.Linq;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var workbook = new Workbook("financial-report-with-metadata.xlsx");
        var properties = workbook.DocumentProperties;

        // Search for properties by name pattern
        Console.WriteLine("=== Properties containing 'Date' ===");
        foreach (var property in properties.Where(p => p.Name.Contains("Date")))
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"{property.Name}: {property.Value}");
        }

        // Find all boolean properties
        Console.WriteLine("\n=== Boolean Properties ===");
        foreach (var property in properties.Where(p => p.Type == PropertyType.Boolean))
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"{property.Name}: {property.Value}");
        }

        // Find all numeric properties
        Console.WriteLine("\n=== Numeric Properties ===");
        foreach (var property in properties.Where(p =>
            p.Type == PropertyType.Int || p.Type == PropertyType.Double))
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"{property.Name}: {property.Value}");
        }

        // Find property by partial name
        Console.WriteLine("\n=== Property Search ===");
        var result = properties.FirstOrDefault(p =>
            p.Name.Equals("ProjectCode", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
        if (result != null)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"Found: {result.Name} = {result.Value}");
        }
    }
}

Important Notes

  • Property Persistence: All properties are saved with the workbook and persist across file loads and saves.
  • Property Limits: There's no strict limit on the number of custom properties, but keep them manageable.
  • Data Types: Use appropriate data types for custom properties to maintain data integrity.
  • Case Sensitivity: Property names are case-sensitive when accessing by name.
  • Property Size: String property values have length limits (typically around 255 characters).
  • Automatic Properties: Some properties (like Pages, Words) are automatically calculated by Excel.
  • Compatibility: Properties are fully compatible with Microsoft Excel.
